question.text: Within how many working days of the closure of a delisting offer must the public announcement regarding the outcome be made?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Within 2 working days of the closure of the delisting offer, a public announcement must be made in the same newspapers disclosing either: (i) success and the final price, (ii) failure of the offer, or (iii) the promoter's rejection of the discovered price. **The Trap:** 1 working day applies to disclosure to exchanges in certain IBC-related delisting scenarios, not to the post-closure public announcement. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX frequently tests specific timelines — the "2 working days" figure recurs in both dispatch of the letter of offer and post-closure announcements. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Same newspapers, same 2-day window — ensure your media buying team has standing arrangements with the publications used for the initial announcement.
